page: Amazon Web Services g6f.4xlarge is a graphics-intensive virtual machine instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.4 GHz. This configuration provides 16 vCPUs across 8 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, a local 450 GB NVMe SSD, and a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps. Acceleration is provided by a fractional NVIDIA L4 GPU delivering 11 GB of VRAM.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core CPU performance, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng tests, while multi-core CPU performance and Geekbench scores remain in the average tier. Memory bandwidth tests reveal a notable performance tradeoff: small-block cached reads perform at average levels, but large-block uncached reads and writes fall into the bottom 25%. However, the integrated NVIDIA L4 GPU enables exceptional performance in specific workloads, achieving top-tier results in the top 10% for LLM inference prompt processing with small and medium models, and strong results in the top 25% for large model prompt processing.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the g6f.4xlarge offers a balanced resource profile for workloads requiring moderate GPU acceleration without the expense of a full dedicated GPU. The inclusion of local NVMe SSD storage and dedicated CPU cores makes it highly suitable for workloads such as low-latency LLM inference, graphics rendering, ray tracing, and database operations that benefit from GPU-assisted processing and fast local storage.
description: Amazon Web Services g6f.4xlarge is a graphics-intensive instance built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features a 16-vCPU AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.4 GHz, 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and a local 450 GB NVMe SSD. GPU acceleration is delivered via a fractional NVIDIA L4 GPU with 11 GB of VRAM. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core CPU execution and top-tier capabilities in LLM inference prompt processing, though large-block memory bandwidth exhibits a performance tradeoff, falling into the bottom tier. With a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps, this instance provides a balanced, cost-efficient hardware profile for workloads requiring moderate GPU acceleration. It is well-suited for applications such as LLM inference, ray tracing, graphics rendering, and database operations.

meta_description: Amazon Web Services g6f.4xlarge features 16 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, a 450 GB NVMe SSD, and a fractional NVIDIA L4 GPU for graphics and LLM inference.
tagline: An accelerated x86 instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing, local NVMe storage, and fractional NVIDIA L4 GPU capabilities.
bullet_points: Powered by a 16-vCPU AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.4 GHz
Equipped with a fractional NVIDIA L4 GPU providing 11 GB of VRAM
Includes 64 GB of DDR4 memory and a local 450 GB NVMe SSD
Delivers top-tier performance for LLM inference prompt processing
Features a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps
Optimized for graphics rendering, ray tracing, and database workloads
